a1.verb_hint: Use do not with I / you / we / they.

questions.question: She {a1} watch TV in the morning
variants: She {a1} watch TV in the morning

questions.question: He does not {a1} to school by bus

a1.verb_hint: After does not, use the base verb.

variants: He does not {a1} to school by bus

questions.question: She does not {a1} books at night

a1.verb_hint: After does not, use the base verb.

variants: She does not {a1} books at night

a1.verb_hint: After does not, use the base verb.
Don't is the short form of do not.
Doesn't is the short form of does not.
Use does not with a singular subject.
Use does not with it / singular nouns.
Use do not with plural subjects.
Use do not with plural nouns.

questions.question: He does not {a1} films on weekdays

a1.verb_hint: After does not, the main verb has no -s.

variants: He does not {a1} films on weekdays

a1.verb_hint: After does not, use study, not studies.
After does not, use base verb.
Negative word order is subject + don't + adverb + base verb.
Doesn't is common with he / she / it.
Team is singular here, so use does not.

a1.verb_hint: The adverb usually sits between do not and the main verb.
Use does not + adverb + base verb.
